problem: What are the first names and surnames of the figure skaters who came 21st in the ice dance category at the 2022 Winter Olympics in Beijing?

response.answer: The figure skaters who came 21st in the ice dance category at the 2022 Winter Olympics in Beijing are Katharina Müller and Tim Dieck from Germany.

problem: What is the name of the kibbutz that Simon Le Bon lived on in 1978?

response.answer: The name of the kibbutz that Simon Le Bon lived on in 1978 is Kibbutz Gvulot, located in the Negev desert in Israel.

problem: Who won the 1991 Ig Nobel Prize for Peace?

response.answer: The 1991 Ig Nobel Prize for Peace was awarded to Edward Teller, father of the hydrogen bomb and first champion of the Star Wars weapons system, "for his lifelong efforts to change the meaning of peace as we know it."

problem: Why did David Chow come to Genoa City on "The Young and the Restless"?
answer: To avenge the murder of his former fiancée, Carmen Mesta.

response.answer: David Chow came to Genoa City on "The Young and the Restless" to bring justice to his former love, Carmen Mesta, by ensuring the police arrested her killer. Carmen had sent David a tape showing Drucilla Winters attacking her, which made David believe Dru was the killer. When he couldn't convince the police, he devised a plan to gas-light Dru using Carmen's lookalike cousin, Inez, which led to Dru being temporarily committed. After the real killer, Jana Hawkes, confessed, David and Inez appeared at Dru's competency hearing to vindicate her. David stayed in Genoa City as a liaison between Granville Global and Newman Enterprises, and later became involved in political campaign management in the city.

problem: In which season and episode of Archer does Sterling go into a rampage? Give me the season, number, and title of the episode.

response.answer: Sterling Archer goes on a rampage in Season 2, Episode 9 of Archer. The episode is titled "Placebo Effect."

problem: On what day, month, and year was Algerian artist Mohammed Racim born?

response.answer: Mohammed Racim, the Algerian artist, was born on 24 June 1896.

problem: How many original artworks were shown in the Society of Illustrators' first "Annual Exhibition"?

response.answer: The Society of Illustrators' first "Annual Exhibition" showed 350 original artworks.
search_results: https://societyillustrators.org/about/history-of-the-society/ - "In 1959, members Bob Peak, Bradbury Thompson and Stevan Dohanos, among others, juried the First Annual Exhibition that resulted in a show of 350 original artworks."

problem: In what year was Eugenius Aristides Nisbet elected to the Georgia House of Representatives?

response.answer: Eugenius Aristides Nisbet was elected to the Georgia House of Representatives in the year 1827.
search_results: From the New Georgia Encyclopedia article on Eugenius A. Nisbet: "In 1827 he was elected to the state House of Representatives and remained there until 1830, when he was elected to the state senate." Source: https://www.georgiaencyclopedia.org/articles/history-archaeology/eugenius-a-nisbet-1803-1871/

problem: What day/month/year was it announced that the politician Ibrahim Rugova had been diagnosed with lung cancer?

response.answer:
Ibrahim Rugova announced that he had been diagnosed with lung cancer on 5 September 2005. This announcement was made publicly on that day, where he stated he had localized lung cancer but would not step down from his position as president.

Sources:
- Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty article dated 5 September 2005 states: "The president of Kosovo, Ibrahim Rugova, said today that he had been diagnosed with lung cancer but will not step down."
- Al Jazeera article dated 21 January 2006 mentions: "Rugova was officially diagnosed with lung cancer last September."
- Wikipedia states: "On 5 September 2005, he announced that he was suffering from lung cancer, but said that he would not be resigning from the post of president."

URLs:
https://www.rferl.org/a/1061163.html
https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2006/1/21/kosovo-president-dies-of-lung-cancer
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ibrahim_Rugova

problem: In 2022, which university did Honda Battle of the Bands (HBOB) select to be the first-ever HBCU campus to host the in-person event?

response.answer: In 2022, Honda Battle of the Bands (HBOB) selected Alabama State University in Montgomery, Alabama to be the first-ever HBCU campus to host the in-person event, which took place in February 2023.

problem: Who wrote the lyrics to "Dream Serenade," the opening theme song for the radio program "The American Album of Familiar Music"?

response.answer: The lyrics to "Dream Serenade," the opening theme song for the radio program "The American Album of Familiar Music," were written by Alfred Bryan. The song was composed by Walter Gustave "Gus" Haenschen.

problem: In what year did Etta Cone last visit Europe?

response.answer: Etta Cone last visited Europe in the year 1938.
search_results: From the Metropolitan Museum of Art's Modern Art Index Project page on Claribel and Etta Cone: "Etta last visited Europe in 1938." Source: https://www.metmuseum.org/research-centers/leonard-a-lauder-research-center/research-resources/modern-art-index-project/cone

problem: What day, month, and year was Vladislav Kaborda born?

response.answer: Vladislav Kaborda was born on 24 July 1995.

problem: In what year was John Williams inducted into the Classical Music Hall of Fame?

response.answer: John Williams was inducted into the American Classical Music Hall of Fame in the year 2004.

problem: On which month and day was the 1985 St. Louis mayoral election held?

response.answer: The 1985 St. Louis mayoral election was held on April 2, 1985.

problem: How many units of the Infanterikanonvagn 72 (1952) were delivered to the Swedish army from 1953 to 1954?

response.answer: From 1953 to 1954, 36 units of the Infanterikanonvagn 72 (1952) were delivered to the Swedish Army.

problem: In which year did Askham Richard, the village in the North of England, first become a conservation area?

response.answer: Askham Richard, the village in the North of England, first became a conservation area in the year 1975. The designation date is specifically recorded as 01 January 1975. This conservation area status embraces the whole village, reflecting its unity of character.

problem: What are the GPS coordinates of Malawi?

response.answer: The GPS coordinates of Malawi are approximately 13° 15' 4.38" S latitude and 34° 18' 5.50" E longitude.

problem: On which day, month, and year did the Hubble Telescope enter a partial safe mode following suspected hardware problems in its most advanced instrument, the Wide Field Camera 3 instrument?

response.answer: The Hubble Space Telescope entered a partial safe mode due to suspected hardware problems in its Wide Field Camera 3 instrument on January 8, 2019, at 17:23 UTC. The instrument autonomously shut down after internal data indicated invalid voltage levels, suspending its operations as a safety precaution.

problem: Before 2013, what was the municipal division of Gusevsky District in Kaliningrad Oblast incorporated as?

response.answer: Before 2013, the municipal division of Gusevsky District in Kaliningrad Oblast was incorporated as Gusevsky Municipal District, which was subdivided into one urban settlement and four rural settlements.

problem: Who was Racing's top scorer in the Copa Libertadores 1967?

response.answer: Racing's top scorer in the Copa Libertadores 1967 was Norberto Raffo, who scored 14 goals in the tournament. He was also the overall top scorer of the competition that year, helping Racing Club to win the title.

problem: What were the date, month, and year of death of the former PM of J&K, Mehr Chand Mahajan?

response.answer: Mehr Chand Mahajan, the former Prime Minister of Jammu and Kashmir, died on 11 December 1967.

problem: What was Beloit College's Center for the Sciences renamed in 2017?
answer: Marjorie and James Sanger Center for the Sciences.

response.answer: In 2017, Beloit College's Center for the Sciences was renamed the "Marjorie and James Sanger Center for the Sciences." This renaming honored the Sanger family's extraordinary leadership and support, particularly Jim Sanger's long service on the board of trustees and their significant contributions to the college, including funding scholarships, new programming, and the construction of the science center building that opened in 2008. The renaming was unanimously recommended by the college’s board of trustees executive committee in October 2017.

problem: From which dealer's shop did the Florentine authorities buy the "Pitti Tondo" in 1823?

response.answer: The Florentine authorities bought the "Pitti Tondo" in 1823 from the shop of the dealer Fedele Acciai.

problem: Who was the director of the Lebedev Physical Institute of the Russian Academy of Sciences between 1951 and 1972?

response.answer: The director of the Lebedev Physical Institute of the Russian Academy of Sciences between 1951 and 1972 was Dmitri Skobeltsyn.
